qa_pairs.question: Should applicants consult with technical staff before submitting a Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) application?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, it is strongly recommended that all applicants consult with technical staff responsible for the clusters they intend to use. This ensures the technical aspects of the proposals match well with the requested clusters, to the Principal Investigator’s (PI’s) benefit.

qa_pairs.question: Who should Principal Investigators (PIs) contact if they haven't used the clusters before?
qa_pairs.answer: PIs who have not previously used our clusters should discuss their proposal with technical staff before submission.

qa_pairs.question: When should consultations with technical staff take place?
qa_pairs.answer: All consultations with staff should take place in advance of completing your proposal to allow time for discussion or revisions if needed.

qa_pairs.question: Who should I contact if I don't know the appropriate technical staff for consultation?
qa_pairs.answer: If you do not know the appropriate technical staff to contact, please email allocations@tech.alliancecan.ca.

qa_pairs.question: How do researchers apply to the Resource Allocation Competition (RAC)?
qa_pairs.answer: Any researcher interested in applying to the RAC must first be registered with the CCDB. All applications must be submitted electronically through the CCDB’s Resource Allocation Competition page.

qa_pairs.question: Can a researcher apply through both the Resources for Research Groups (RRG) and the Research Platforms and Portals (RPP) processes?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes. A Principal Investigator (PI) can apply as a lead submitter for only one RRG application but can be involved in multiple RRG submissions as a participant. PIs may simultaneously hold lead positions on one or several RPP projects but only one RRG project.

qa_pairs.question: What should applicants do before submitting a proposal for RRG or RPP?
qa_pairs.answer: Prior to submitting a proposal, applicants should review each competition’s criteria to ensure their project aligns with the focus.

qa_pairs.question: Who should I contact if I have questions after reviewing the competition guide?
qa_pairs.answer: If you still have questions or concerns after reviewing the competition guide, please contact us at allocations@tech.alliancecan.ca.

qa_pairs.question: Is the Canadian Common CV (CCV) required for a RAC application?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, Principal Investigators (PIs) and all Co-PIs of a RAC application are required to submit a CCV.

qa_pairs.question: Why is integrating the CCV into the CCDB beneficial for RAC applications?
qa_pairs.answer: Integrating CCV into the CCDB makes it easier for applicants to upload their CVs and standardize their format. It also helps the Scientific Review Committees evaluate the quality of each project’s research team.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find instructions on how to submit a CCV?
qa_pairs.answer: For instructions on how to submit a CCV, read the Alliance CCV submission guide.

qa_pairs.question: How can Co-PIs update their CCV on the CCDB?
qa_pairs.answer: Co-PIs can update their CCV on the CCDB by clicking on the "Update CCV" button in the Resource Applications page, or by going to My Account → View Reporting.

qa_pairs.question: Is a glossary available for acronyms or terms used in the RAC documentation?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, a list of key terms used in the competition and application process is available. Please consult the Technical Glossary when preparing your application.

qa_pairs.question: Will my project proposal be at a disadvantage if it doesn’t rely on, or only partly overlaps with, existing Advanced Research Computing (ARC) infrastructure?
qa_pairs.answer: The Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) only grants access to ARC infrastructure.

qa_pairs.question: Who should I contact if I have questions about whether my infrastructure is part of the national platform?
qa_pairs.answer: If you have questions about whether the infrastructure you are using is part of the national platform, please contact us at allocations@tech.alliancecan.ca.

qa_pairs.question: Can academic Principal Investigators (PIs) with small research teams or limited funding still apply to the RAC?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, academic PIs can apply for an allocation, irrespective of the size of the research team or the funding received.

qa_pairs.question: Can the deadlines for final RAC application submissions be extended?
qa_pairs.answer: No, the submission deadline for RAC applications is final. There will be no extensions, and late submissions will not be accepted.

qa_pairs.question: Who should I contact if I have concerns about the RAC application deadline policy?
qa_pairs.answer: Any concerns with this policy may be directed to allocations@tech.alliancecan.ca.

qa_pairs.question: When will applicants be notified of the results of their RAC application?
qa_pairs.answer: All applicants will be notified of their competition results in March.

qa_pairs.question: How will successful applicants be contacted and what information will they receive?
qa_pairs.answer: Successful applicants will be contacted by email with any additional information you may need or action items required of you to finalize your allocation.

qa_pairs.question: Where will a list of RAC recipients and allocation details be published?
qa_pairs.answer: Once all recipients have been privately notified, a list of recipients, project allocation amounts, and allocation values will be published on the RAC Annual Reports page.

qa_pairs.question: How is the monetary value of RAC allocations calculated?
qa_pairs.answer: The values represent an average across all our facilities and include the total capital and operational costs incurred by the Federation to deliver the resources and associated services. These are not commercial or market values.

qa_pairs.question: Can the in-kind monetary valuation of a RAC allocation be used as a match for a CFI award?
qa_pairs.answer: No, this in-kind valuation cannot be used as a match for a CFI award.

qa_pairs.question: What is the purpose of assigning a monetary value to each RAC allocation?
qa_pairs.answer: Assigning a monetary value is useful for researchers to assign an in-kind value of the contribution of the Federation to their research program.

qa_pairs.question: Why might a final allocation be less than the requested amount, even if the impact of a reduction was explained?
qa_pairs.answer: Each year, our infrastructure is heavily oversubscribed. A scaling function is applied to compute requests to provide a means by which decisions on allocations in a context of insufficient capacity can be made.
